title: template.v1 — the shape a surface template declares
description: A template gives an engine prose surface its starting structure and its checkable shape in one file: a prose skeleton (the headings an author starts from) plus this shape-spec, which declares the sections an instance must and may contain and a gentle length budget. This schema governs the shape-spec itself — it checks that the shape-spec is well-formed. It does not, on its own, check any instance against it; that comparison is done by the engine's validation engine. A surface's template lives where that surface's catalog entry points to it.

required_sections.description: The sections every instance written from this template must contain, each named once. The order they are listed in is the order the sections are expected to appear in.

allowed_sections.description: Optional sections an instance may include in addition to the required ones, each named once. A section that is neither required nor allowed is not part of this template's shape.

length_budget.description: A gentle maximum length, in lines, for an instance's body. Going over it is a nudge to trim, never a refusal — length is guidance, not a hard limit.
